Organic Acid Disorders: It was indicated that the infant is either low birth weight, on TPN or Hyperalimentation. We can not reliably determine the significance of the out of range marker(s). A repeat dried spot specimen should be properly collected and resubmitted to the Newborn Screening Laboratory in Jacksonville immediately after the infant has been removed from TPN or hyperalimentation for at least 48 hours.
This infant may be at risk for an inborn error of metabolism. Another specimen is required. A REPEAT dried blood specimen should be properly collected and resubmitted to the Newborn Screening Program Laboratory in Jacksonville immediately.
Biotinidase Deficiency: The results indicate a LOW ENZYME ACTIVITY OF BIOTINIDASE , which suggests Biotinidase Deficiency. This patient must have additional testing and evaluation to confirm or rule out a diagnosis.  This patient has been referred to CMS.  Contact NBS Follow-Up for further assistance: 866-804-9166.
Hypothyroidism: The result indicates LOW T4 which may suggest Congenital Hypothyroidism. Prematurity, severe illness, thyroxine binding globulin deficiency and hypopituitarism may cause these findings. Repeat screen at 2-3 weeks or test for free T4 and TSH at a local laboratory to confirm results. Contact NBS Follow-up for assistance 866-804-9166.
CAH: The result for 17-hydroxyprogesterone (17-OHP) indicates a Borderline level, which may suggest Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia. Examine infant for ambiguous genitalia or signs of the salt-wasting variant. Send a specimen collected at 2 weeks of age or before discharge to the NBS Program Lab for repeat testing of 17-OHP. Contact NBS Follow-Up for further assistance: (850)245-4201 or (866)804-9166.
Hemoglobinopathies: Indicates possible transfusion or blood is from an older child with presence of an unknown hemoglobin. Repeat hemoglobinopathy testing through a clinical laboratory in 120 days after the last transfusion.  Clinical correlation is indicated.
Cystic Fibrosis: Out of range
SCID: The result suggests this infant may be at risk for SCID or other immune deficiency disorder. This infant has been referred by NBS Follow-up to a SCID Referral Center for diagnostic testing. Repeat screening is not advised. Contact NBS Follow-up Program for further assistance and prior to administering the Rotavirus or other live vaccine at 866-804-9166.
Organic Acid Disorders:  - Abnormal
XALD: The results suggest that the infant may be at risk for a peroxisomal disorder. This infant has been referred to the Genetics Referral Center in your area. Repeat specimen is not advised. Contact NBS Follow-up for the supplemental biochemical and DNA sequencing results or for further assistance at (866) 804-9166.
Organic Acid Disorders:  - Abnormal
LSD: The results suggest that the infant may be at risk for Pompe Disease. This infant has been referred by NBS Follow-up to the Genetic Referral Center for further diagnostic testing. Repeat specimen is not advised. Contact NBS Follow-up for the supplemental DNA Sequencing results or for further assistance at 866-804-9166.
SMA: This screening result suggests that the infant may be at risk for SMA based on identification of a homozygous deletion of exon 7 in the SMN1 gene. This infant has been referred by NBS Follow-up to the Genetic Referral Center for further diagnostic testing. Repeat specimen is not advised. Contact NBS Follow-up for further assistance at 866-804-9166.
Organic Acid Disorders:  - Abnormal
